edges.description: TSEN-complex defects converge on prenatal pontocerebellar maldevelopment with progressive atrophy.
edges.source: TSEN/CLP1 Dysfunction and Impaired Pre-tRNA Splicing
edges.target: Impaired Cerebellar Development and Progressive Degeneration

edges.description: EXOSC3-related PCH1B converges on pontocerebellar hypoplasia and progressive neurodevelopmental impairment.
edges.target: Impaired Cerebellar Development and Progressive Degeneration

edges.description: RARS2-related mitochondrial translation defects are associated with progressive pontocerebellar and cortical atrophy.
edges.target: Impaired Cerebellar Development and Progressive Degeneration

edges.description: VRK1-associated SMA-PCH combines anterior horn cell degeneration with pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
edges.target: Impaired Cerebellar Development and Progressive Degeneration
TSEN/CLP1 Dysfunction and Impaired Pre-tRNA Splicing

nodes.description: In PCH1A, biallelic VRK1 loss-of-function mutations impair nuclear envelope formation and RNA processing in post-mitotic neurons. Motor neurons of the anterior horn of the spinal cord are additionally affected beyond pontocerebellar neurons, producing a spinal muscular atrophy-like clinical picture (hypotonia, muscle weakness, areflexia) superimposed on pontocerebellar hypoplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Spinal muscular atrophy with pontocerebellar hypoplasia is caused by a mutation in the VRK1 gene.
evidence.snippet: identified a nonsense mutation in the vaccinia-related kinase 1 gene (VRK1) as a cause of SMA-PCH

evidence.reference_title: Spinal muscular atrophy with pontocerebellar hypoplasia is caused by a mutation in the VRK1 gene.
evidence.snippet: VRK1, one of three members of the mammalian VRK family, is a serine/threonine kinase that phosphorylates p53 and CREB and is essential for nuclear envelope formation.

nodes.description: Convergent downstream consequence of TSEN complex dysfunction, mitochondrial tRNA synthetase deficiency, and motor neuron degeneration. Pontocerebellar neurons fail to develop normally in utero (prenatal onset), followed by progressive postnatal degeneration. Neuropathology shows reduced cerebellar volume, simplified or absent foliation, and hypoplastic pons with loss of pontine nuclei. The cerebral cortex is relatively spared. MRI shows the characteristic dragonfly or butterfly sign of hypoplastic cerebellar hemispheres with preserved vermis in PCH2.
nodes.id: Impaired Cerebellar Development and Progressive Degeneration

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neuroradiological and genetic findings in pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: The common characteristics are cerebellar hypoplasia with variable atrophy of the cerebellum and the ventral pons.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neuroradiological and genetic findings in pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: We found a strong correlation (P < 0.0005) between TSEN54 mutations and a dragonfly-like cerebellar pattern on magnetic resonance imaging, in which the cerebellar hemispheres are flat and severely reduced in size and the vermis is relatively spared.

nodes.description: Mutations in RARS2, encoding the mitochondrial arginyl-tRNA synthetase, reduce aminoacylation of mitochondrial tRNA-Arg. This impairs mitochondrial translation, decreases oxidative phosphorylation capacity, and leads to energy failure preferentially in high-metabolic-demand cells of the developing cerebellum and pons. Elevated CSF lactate and reduced respiratory chain complex activity are biochemical hallmarks.

biological_processes: tRNA aminoacylation for mitochondrial protein translation

evidence.reference_title: Deleterious mutation in the mitochondrial arginyl-transfer RNA synthetase gene is associated with pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: identification of an intronic mutation in RARS2, the gene encoding mitochondrial arginine-transfer RNA (tRNA) synthetase

evidence.reference_title: Deleterious mutation in the mitochondrial arginyl-transfer RNA synthetase gene is associated with pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: The mutation was associated with the production of an abnormally short RARS2 transcript and a marked reduction of the mitochondrial tRNA(Arg) transcript in the patients' fibroblasts.

evidence.reference_title: Pontocerebellar hypoplasia type 6 caused by mutations in RARS2: definition of the clinical spectrum and molecular findings in five patients.
evidence.snippet: Blood and CSF lactate was abnormally elevated in all five patients at early stages

nodes.description: Human induced pluripotent stem cell lines from individuals homozygous for the TSEN54 p.Ala307Ser founder variant were differentiated into cerebellar and neocortical organoids. The model recapitulates the region-specific growth deficit of PCH2A and supports altered progenitor proliferation kinetics as an early neurodevelopmental mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Human organoid model of pontocerebellar hypoplasia 2a recapitulates brain region-specific size differences
evidence.snippet: we developed human models of PCH2a using regionalized neural organoids.

nodes.description: Biallelic EXOSC3 mutations impair RNA exosome function and cause the PCH1B subtype. This mechanism produces pontocerebellar hypoplasia with spinal muscular atrophy-like weakness and global developmental delay, but survival and clinical severity vary by genotype.

evidence.reference_title: Pontocerebellar hypoplasia type 1: clinical spectrum and relevance of EXOSC3 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Pontocerebellar hypoplasia with spinal muscular atrophy, also known as PCH1, is a group of autosomal recessive disorders characterized by generalized muscle weakness and global developmental delay commonly resulting in early death.

evidence.reference_title: Pontocerebellar hypoplasia type 1: clinical spectrum and relevance of EXOSC3 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic mutations in EXOSC3 were detected in 10 of 27 families (37%).

nodes.description: The tRNA splicing endonuclease (TSEN) complex, composed of TSEN2, TSEN15, TSEN34, and TSEN54, cleaves introns from pre-tRNA molecules at the canonical anticodon loop position. Mutations in TSEN54 (and other TSEN subunits) impair pre-tRNA processing, reducing the pool of mature, functional tRNAs available for translation. Developing pontocerebellar neurons have exceptionally high translational demands and are disproportionately vulnerable. This mechanism maps primarily to PCH2/PCH4 for TSEN genes and to PCH10 for CLP1. The TSEN complex also interacts with CLP1, an RNA kinase, reinforcing RNA-processing as the central pathogenic pathway.
nodes.id: TSEN/CLP1 Dysfunction and Impaired Pre-tRNA Splicing

biological_processes: tRNA splicing via endonucleolytic cleavage and ligation

evidence.reference_title: tRNA splicing endonuclease mutations cause pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: In two subtypes, PCH2 and PCH4, we identified mutations in three of the four different subunits of the tRNA-splicing endonuclease complex.

evidence.reference_title: tRNA splicing endonuclease mutations cause pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: Our findings point to RNA processing as a new basic cellular impairment in neurological disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Classification, diagnosis and potential mechanisms in pontocerebellar hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in three tRNA splicing endonuclease subunit genes were found to be responsible for PCH2, PCH4 and PCH5.

evidence.reference_title: Human CLP1 mutations alter tRNA biogenesis, affecting both peripheral and central nervous system function.
evidence.snippet: Human genome analyses now identified a CLP1 homozygous missense mutation (p.R140H) in five unrelated families, leading to a loss of CLP1 interaction with the tRNA splicing endonuclease (TSEN) complex, largely reduced pre-tRNA cleavage activity, and accumulation of linear tRNA introns.

experimental_models.description: Recapitulates TSEN54-related, brain-region-specific organoid growth reduction and altered neural progenitor proliferation.
experimental_models.name: PCH2A Patient-Derived Regional Neural Organoids
